title: Minecraft: Frog Edition
description: A fun and cute adventure modpack
body:
## Minecraft: Frog Edition is a _**cute**_ survival adventure modpack with something for everyone, designed for a casual-friendly experience for new players and veterans as well.

Featuring "cute" wildlife creatures, [Mythic Mounts](https://modrinth.com/mod/mythic-mounts), [Beautiful Nature](https://modrinth.com/mod/natures-spirit), expanded sea life, plenty of home decorative options, collectible plushies, cosmetic hats, and many ways for social interactions.

And, of course, [cute frogs](https://modrinth.com/mod/ribbits).

Additionally, featuring [Better Combat](https://modrinth.com/mod/better-combat), allowing you to choose and become one of many classes such as **Paladin**, **Priest**, **Rogue**, **Ranger**, **Warrior**, **Wizard**, **Death Knight** and more, with a large skill tree, many magical abilities, and naturally also having awesome **dungeons** and **bosses** as well, all optional of course.

This modpack also includes the mod [Create](https://modrinth.com/mod/create-fabric), which enables players to create mechanical contraptions and automation, like for example windmills, trains, elevators and much more.

And thanks to the mods from the [Let's do](https://modrinth.com/mod/lets-do-farm-charm) series, the modpack has immersive and expansive cooking and food making.

This modpack supports shaders!
You can access the shader menu in settings, or by pressing **F9** while ingame and clicking Enable at the top center.

We hope you enjoy!


**Default Keybindings:**

<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

**K** Skill Tree
(The skill tree do NOT use normal experience, and is instead using its own experience leveling system, that does not vanish upon death)

**M** = World Map

**N** = Create Waypoint Marker

**R** = Combat Roll               (4 second cooldown on use)

**F** = Swap Item with Offhand    (same as vanilla)

**Q** = Drop Item    (Hold Q to throw item further, hold it longer for greater throw distance)

**B** = Access Equipped Backpack

**Z** = Use equipped Spyglass (you can use scroll wheel to zoom in or out)

**Left Shift + Right-Click** (with both hands empty) to pick up small mobs, players, chests etc.

**F7** = Enlarge Minimap

**F8** = Mod Menu

**F9** = Shaders Menu

(Disclaimer: the shader graphic settings are set to **Potato** by default, it is recommended to change it to **Medium** for best immersive experience)

Hold **Left Shift** while in inventory to compare items.

**G** spell 1

**V** spell 2

**C** spell 3

**Y** spell 4

**H** Signature Spell

**X** Ascendancy Spell

</details>

**How to Play (Starting Tips)**:


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

First of all, if playing on **Multiplayer**, you will want to create a **Party**, so that magical abilities do not harm friends, and making sure healing abilities actually heal friends.

You can create a party by typing in chat the following command:

**/openpac-parties create**


After creating a party, you can invite others with the following command:

**/openpac-parties member invite [player name]**



While in the **inventory**, you will see a **search bar** to the right, as well as items above it.
You can search for individual items, then **left-click** the item to see how **to craft** it, or **right-click** the item to see what you **can craft** from said item.
You can also use @ on the search bar, followed up by a mod name, to see all items from said mod, for example **@waystones** or **@sophisticatedbackpacks** (remove space between mod name)


Second, while at the **Inventory**, you will see a small button next to your player model in the center, and while hovering over it should say "open accessories", pressing it will take you to your **accessory menu**

The **accessory menu** is where you equip stuff like **backpacks**, **cosmetic hats**, **spell books** and much more.

You can also **hide** the visual part of accessories by pressing the **tiny button** on the top right corner of each slot, hidden accessories will still function, they are just invisible.



**Waystones** will randomly appear all over the world, and sometimes on top of large towers, and sometimes there will also be one inside a village.

Players can **right-click** to activate a **Waystone**, and can then **Teleport** between **Waystones**.
(Players riding mounts will teleport said mount as well)



**Backpacks** are awesome external inventories that a new players should seek to obtain as soon as possible.

**Backpacks** can be equipped in the **accessory menu**, and you can open the backpack by pressing **B**.


**Spyglass** no longer need to be equipped, **Spyglass** can now be used from your inventory by pressing **Z**.


A **Lantern** can be placed in the **Belt** slot in your **Acceessory Menu**, giving you a constant light source around you.



</details>

**How to Play (Combat)**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>


You can access the **Skill Tree** by pressing **K**, and you will earn **Skill Points** by slaying enemies, and can then spend said points on upgrades.

Now, to obtain and be able to use **Spells**, you will need to create a **Spell Book**, and you can do this by:

First, crafting a **Spell Binding Table**, placing it down, and surround it with **Bookshelfs**, similar to a **Enchantment Table**.

Placing a regular book inside a **Spell Binding Table** will allow you to turn the book into a **Spell Book**, for example: **Book of Fire**.

You can then upgrade your **Skill Book** by placing it inside the **Spell Binding Table**, and selecting a **spell** that you want, which costs **Lapis Lazuli** and a large amount of regular **Experience Levels**

You can then equip your **Spell Book** inside your **Accessories Menu**, and will then be able to use said abilities while wielding a **weapon**.

**Spells** requires a specific **Rune** each time when cast, for example: if having **Book of Fire**, you will need **Fire Runes** to cast the fire spells.



Also, killing Monsters will sometimes drop **Hat Bags**

These **Hat Bags** can be opened by **right-clicking** whilst holding it, to obtain a random **cosmetic hat**, which can be equipped in your **Accessory Menu**



</details>


**Additional Information:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

Upon **Death**, you will now keep **Weapons**, **Armor**, **Ammo**, **Trinkets** and **Gadgets** for example **Backpacks** or **Cosmetic Hats**, however you will still **drop blocks, gems etc** upon death, as well as loose all experience.


Dropped items will now decay after **10 minutes**, instead of the normal **5 minutes**.


Items with the [Mending](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Mending) enchantment can now be manually repaired by simply **holding** the item, **Sneaking** and **Right-Clicking** to directly transfer some of your experience into repairing the held item.


You can now place a enchanted item on a **Grindstone**, and a regular book, to **transfer** the enchantments from the item into the book.


You can change the **Enchantment Color** on enchanted items now, by simply typing the following command:

**/glint_color [color]**

Additional glint commands can be found [here.](https://modrinth.com/mod/no-more-purple)


</details>


**Known Bugs/Issues:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

[Bug] If textures appear **black**, for example the top side of chests, use the following command in-game:

**/flywheel backened instancing**

That command should fix the issue.
This bug "should" not appear while using **Shaders**.


[Bug] Sometimes the "item frame" around the item name will glitch out, this happens sometimes when going in/out of the **Accessories Menu**.

Simply go to the **Accessories Menu** again, and look at an item in your own inventory, then exit the **Accessories Menu**, and it should be fixed.

 
[Issue] Sometimes the loot beams will bug and glitch out, there is no known fix :c


[Issue] While in the **Accessory Menu**, using the **mouse scroll wheel** while hovering over equipped items will **Unequip them**, keep this in mind as it is very easy to accidentally unequip gear when using the mouse scroll wheel.


</details>
